A regression test script for issue 281 in the JerryScript engine.

# Purpose
This code is a simple script that demonstrates the use of a `while` loop with a condition that is always `false`. The loop contains a single statement, `foo`, which will never execute because the condition `false` prevents the loop from starting. This script serves as an example of a loop structure that is effectively inert due to its condition. It does not provide any broad functionality or perform any operations beyond illustrating the behavior of a `while` loop with a constant false condition.
